name: Contested Divorce Checklist
description: Action steps for filing and managing a contested/disputed divorce case

base_steps.action: Consult family law attorney specialized in divorce
details: Interview 2-3 family law attorneys and compare their approach
Discuss grounds for divorce (adultery, cruelty, desertion, etc.)
Understand timeline (typically 6-18 months for contested))
Ask about complexity and estimated legal costs
Arrange fee payment structure and retainer
Prepare detailed written account of cruelty/abandonment with dates and witnesses
Collect all evidence (letters, emails, photographs, medical reports)
Get witness statements in writing from people who witnessed incidents
Compile any communications from spouse (letters, messages) that support grounds
Organize evidence chronologically in separate folders/files
Send formal notice to spouse mentioning reconciliation attempt
Attend reconciliation meeting with or without mediator
Document all reconciliation attempts with dates and details
Get mutual agreement if reconciliation succeeds, else proceed with divorce

base_steps.action: File divorce petition in district court
details: Prepare divorce petition with lawyer's help
Pay court filing fees
Serve summons to spouse (personally or through court)
Spouse has 30 days to file response
Obtain court date for first hearing
Prepare list of all assets and liabilities
Calculate fair share of property and savings
Discuss alimony/maintenance if applicable
Agree on custody and visitation of children
Negotiate settlement or prepare for contested court battle
Never miss scheduled court dates
Bring all required documents
Follow lawyer's guidance on what to say in court
Be prepared to answer questions from judge or opponent's lawyer
Document all court proceedings and orders

India.action: Seek court protection order if needed
details: Apply for maintenance during pendency of case under Section 24
Request custody order for children under Section 26
Apply for protection order if subject to harassment/abuse
Request interim protection while case is pending
